sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Energy developers head to court as Trump says the US 'will not approve any windmills'
feed.summary: Three energy developers, Orsted, Equinor, and Dominion Energy Virginia, are challenging the Trump administration's freeze on their offshore wind projects in court. This comes after President Donald Trump stated that the U.S. "will not approve any windmills," citing national security concerns, economic unviability, and environmental impact. The companies argue the freeze is arbitrary, unconstitutional, and is disrupting their construction schedules.

feed.title: Establishment Labs expects 27% revenue growth for fiscal 2025 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:ESTA) anticipates a 27% revenue growth for fiscal year 2025, projecting full-year revenue between $210.5 million and $211.5 million. The medical technology company expects Q4 revenue of $64.0 million to $65.0 million, with a strong cash position of approximately $75.5 million by year-end. Despite not being currently profitable, analysts maintain positive outlooks and have raised price targets, especially after the company's FDA application for Motiva implants in breast reconstruction.

feed.title: Acer Sues AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile Over Mobile-Network Patents
feed.summary: Acer Inc. has filed patent-infringement lawsuits against AT&T Inc., T-Mobile Inc., and Verizon Wireless, alleging that these carriers are using six of Acer's patents in their mobile-network infrastructure without authorization. The patents in question are described as standard essential patents, integrated into wireless standards like LTE and 5G. Acer claims it had previously offered licensing agreements to the mobile carriers for the use of these technologies.

feed.title: Strable-Soethout sells Principal Financial (PFG) shares for $27,750 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Deanna D. Strable-Soethout, President and CEO of Principal Financial Group Inc. (NASDAQ: PFG), sold 300 shares of the company's common stock for $27,750 on January 8, 2026, under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. This transaction occurred shortly after the company reported strong Q3 2025 earnings, surpassing analyst EPS expectations, though JPMorgan recently downgraded the stock from Overweight to Neutral.

feed.title: Valero Energy Corp: How a Quiet Refining Giant Became a High-Performance Energy Platform
feed.summary: Valero Energy Corp, traditionally a refining and midstream company, has transformed into a sophisticated, high-performance energy platform. It achieves this through complex, flexible refining, industrial-scale low-carbon fuels production (renewable diesel and ethanol), and an export-centric logistics network. This strategic evolution provides a competitive edge, driving strong cash generation and making Valero a compelling investment in the energy sector.

feed.title: Tesla Inc. (TSLA) Wells Fargo Maintains Bearish Forecast
feed.summary: Wells Fargo analyst Colin Langan has reaffirmed an Underweight (Sell-equivalent) rating for Tesla Inc. (TSLA), despite an upward revision of the 12-month price target to $130 from $120. The firm remains bearish due to concerns over slowing vehicle demand, intensifying competition, margin pressure, and a belief that Tesla's valuation is disconnected from its near-term earnings power, implying a significant downside from its current share price.

feed.title: MACOM stock jumps 11% after MTSI adds ex-Broadcom wireless executive to board
feed.summary: MACOM shares surged 11% after the chipmaker announced the appointment of former Broadcom executive Bryan Ingram to its board of directors. The stock's performance reflects investor optimism ahead of MACOM's upcoming earnings report, where insights into demand and margins in the telecom and data center sectors will be crucial. Ingram's extensive background in wireless semiconductors and supply chain is seen as a significant asset for the company.

feed.title: Apple taps Google AI to power Siri in multi-year deal
feed.summary: Google has confirmed a multi-year agreement with Apple to power the iPhone-maker's artificial intelligence technology, including its Siri voice assistant. Apple will use Google's Gemini AI model to enhance Siri's capabilities while it develops its own AI systems. Wedbush analysts view this as a significant milestone, validating Google's AI and providing Apple with a crucial stepping stone for its AI strategy, potentially generating new subscription-based revenue.

feed.title: JP Morgan Raises Price Target for C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW)
feed.summary: JP Morgan has maintained its "Overweight" rating for C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) and raised its price target by 7.23% to $178.00. This is part of a trend where several analysts have recently increased their price targets for CHRW while keeping their ratings stable. The article also provides details on CHRW's business operations and Wall Street's consensus forecast, which indicates an "Outperform" status despite a potential downside based on GuruFocus's GF Value estimate.

feed.title: Union Pacific (UNP) Analyst Rating Update: JP Morgan Raises Price Target | UNP Stock News
feed.summary: JP Morgan has maintained a Neutral rating on Union Pacific (UNP) but increased its price target from $267 to $270, representing a 1.12% rise. This adjustment signals a slightly positive outlook on UNP's future. The article also provides a summary of recent historical analyst ratings and a consensus forecast from 25 analysts, indicating an average target price of $261.22 with an "Outperform" status.

feed.title: CoStar Group (CSGP): Analyst Lowers Price Target to $75 | CSGP S
feed.summary: Keefe, Bruyette & Woods has maintained its "Outperform" rating for CoStar Group (CSGP) but lowered its price target from $100 to $75. This adjustment follows a trend of several analysts reducing their price targets for CSGP. Despite these reductions, the average target price from 16 analysts is $82.34, implying a 41.29% upside from the current price, and GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $116.61, suggesting a 100.09% upside.

feed.title: Will Delta (DAL) Beat Estimates Again in Its Next Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines (DAL) is positioned for a potential earnings beat in its upcoming report, scheduled for January 13, 2026. The company has a history of surpassing earnings estimates, with an average surprise of 7.72% over the last two quarters. With a Zacks Earnings ESP of +0.24% and a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), the indicators suggest a favorable outcome.

feed.title: Why Shares of Capital One Are Sinking Today
feed.summary: Shares of Capital One (NYSE: COF) fell by nearly 6.5% after President Donald Trump announced a potential one-year, 10% cap on credit card interest rates, set to take effect on January 20th. This move, aimed at improving affordability ahead of midterm elections, raised concerns among industry groups about reduced credit availability and its impact on economic growth. The author, however, views the sell-off as a buying opportunity, believing the interest rate cap is unlikely to be implemented or will be short-lived.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Calls Capital One "Absolutely Terrific"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Capital One Financial Corporation (NYSE: COF) as an "absolutely terrific" stock, noting its strong performance within the financials sector and inclusion in the Charitable Trust's holdings. Despite its positive outlook for banks, the article suggests that certain AI stocks might offer greater upside potential with less risk. This comes amidst broader discussions on the financials sector's performance and specific banks doing well.

feed.title: Nation’s Largest Off-Price Retail Chain Finds New Home in Poughkeepsie
feed.summary: Ross Dress for Less, the largest off-price retail chain in the U.S., is set to open a new 23,000-square-foot store in Poughkeepsie's Plaza 44 by the end of 2026. This new store will occupy the space previously held by a Big Lots location that closed in August 2024 due to the company's Chapter 11 bankruptcy. Ross Dress for Less offers name-brand apparel, footwear, accessories, and home goods at significant discounts by utilizing a "no-frills" business model.

feed.title: JP Morgan 2026: Bristol Myers Squibb Focuses on the Next Decade
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb's CEO Christopher Boerner outlined the company's future growth strategy at the JP Morgan Healthcare Conference, focusing on diversifying its drug portfolio across neuroscience, cardiovascular, immunology, and oncology. The plan emphasizes strategic partnerships, financial discipline, commercial execution, and pipeline development to overcome upcoming patent cliffs. Recent positive results from the SCOUT-HCM trial for Camzyos underscore the potential for new advancements in treating adolescent obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

feed.title: Cummins (NYSE:CMI) Hits New 12-Month High - What's Next?
feed.summary: Cummins (NYSE:CMI) reached a new 52-week high, trading up to $555.98 with strong intraday momentum and trading volume. Analyst sentiment is generally positive with a "Moderate Buy" consensus and several price target increases. Despite recent solid earnings results, there has been notable insider selling totaling approximately 19,330 shares within the last 90 days.

feed.title: Nio Stock Is Rising Monday: What Investors Are Watching
feed.summary: Nio (NYSE: NIO) stock is moving higher on Monday following an announcement that China and the European Union have reached an agreement on price undertakings for Chinese battery electric vehicle exports. This agreement aims to provide guidance for Chinese EV makers to submit price-undertaking offers, potentially replacing current tariffs. Despite recent struggles with key moving averages, the stock has shown a 20.44% gain over the last 12 months, indicating a longer-term upward trend.

feed.title: Why DexCom Is the Top Stock in the S&P 500 Today
feed.summary: DexCom's stock experienced a significant rise today, making it the top performer in the S&P 500. This surge follows the company's announcement of preliminary fourth-quarter results that exceeded expectations. DexCom is known for manufacturing glucose-monitoring systems.

feed.title: Granite Construction stock hits all-time high at 122.85 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Granite Construction Inc. (GVA) stock reached an all-time high of $122.85, reflecting a 38.58% appreciation over the past year. This surge is supported by investor confidence, 36 consecutive years of dividend payments, and a "GOOD" financial health score, despite InvestingPro suggesting the stock may be overvalued. The company recently exceeded Q3 2025 EPS expectations, secured a $24 million contract, and declared a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Emergent BioSolutions Announces $100 Million Paydown of Term Loan Principal, a Significant Step Achieved Toward its Multi-year Transformation
feed.summary: Emergent BioSolutions announced a voluntary prepayment of $100 million on its term loan facility in late December 2025, using cash on hand. This action reduces the company's gross debt by $275 million since 2023, totaling $593 million, representing a 32 percent decline since the start of its multi-year transformation plan. This move aims to improve Emergent's financial position, enhance flexibility, and support long-term stability and growth.

feed.title: Commonwealth Fusion Systems Appoints Stephane Bancel To Board
feed.summary: Commonwealth Fusion Systems (CFS), a leading private fusion company, announced that Stephane Bancel, Moderna Inc.'s CEO, has joined its Board of Directors as an Independent Member. Bancel, known for leading Moderna's COVID-19 vaccine development, brings expertise in scaling innovative technologies. He was also an early investor in CFS and aims to help commercialize fusion energy for global power demand.

feed.title: Worldpay, one of Cincinnati’s largest companies, gets acquired in biggest local deal in six-plus years
feed.summary: Worldpay, a major company based in Cincinnati, has been acquired, marking the largest business deal in the local area in over six years. The company will continue to maintain its office in Symmes Township. This acquisition is a significant development for one of Greater Cincinnati's most prominent businesses.

feed.title: The National Observer: City braces for HQ relocation in major corporate departure
feed.summary: This article from The National Observer discusses the impact of a major corporate headquarters relocation on a city's downtown area. It also touches on how educators and employers are addressing a perceived skills gap among future employees. The piece suggests a community will soon experience firsthand the effects of such a significant corporate departure.

feed.title: Will Enphase Energy (ENPH) Beat Estimates Again in Its Next Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Enphase Energy (ENPH) has a history of consistently beating earnings estimates, with an average surprise of 28.23% over the last two quarters. Analysts are optimistic about its next report, giving the company a positive Zacks Earnings ESP of +10.63% and a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), suggesting another earnings beat is likely. Investors are advised to utilize the Earnings ESP Filter for informed decisions.

feed.title: The Next Phase of Data Center Growth
feed.summary: The rise of AI is significantly transforming data center requirements, demanding higher compute densities and leading to massive capital expenditures that will soon exceed even hyperscalers' annual CapEx. This shift creates new pressures on infrastructure design, siting, and economics, with critical factors like power procurement and rapid deployment becoming decisive competitive advantages. Investors and operators must critically assess tenant contracts, RFS timelines, obsolescence risks, demand emergence, and alternative power sources to navigate this evolving landscape and remain competitive.

feed.title: Stifel reiterates Buy rating on Foghorn Therapeutics stock at $12 target
feed.summary: Stifel has reaffirmed its Buy rating on Foghorn Therapeutics (NASDAQ: FHTX) with a $12 price target, citing the company's recent $50 million equity financing and updates on its programs. This financing, priced at a 30% premium, extends Foghorn's cash runway into the first half of 2028, supporting its clinical pipeline, including the FHD-909 SMARCA2 inhibitor. Foghorn is also advancing its wholly owned selective degrader pipeline, with initial data for FHD-909 and a potential Eli Lilly decision expected in the first half of 2026.

feed.title: Forum Energy Technologies appoints Leslie Beyer to board of directors
feed.summary: Forum Energy Technologies, Inc. (NYSE:FET) has appointed Leslie A. Beyer to its Board of Directors. Beyer, who previously served at the U.S. Department of Interior and as CEO of the Energy Workforce and Technology Council, will also serve on the company's Compensation & Human Capital Committee and the Nominating, Governance & Sustainability Committee. Her extensive government and industry experience is expected to bring valuable insights to Forum Energy Technologies.

feed.title: FIS closes Global Payments’ Issuer Solutions acquisition
feed.summary: Financial technology firm FIS has announced the completion of its acquisition of Global Payments’ Issuer Solutions business, formerly known as TSYS. The deal was valued at $13.5 billion, with a net purchase price of $12 billion after accounting for tax assets. The transaction closed ahead of schedule, marking a significant move for FIS in the financial technology sector.

feed.title: CVRx expects 11-18% revenue growth in 2026 following CPT code transition
feed.summary: CVRx, Inc., a medical device company, projects its 2026 revenue to be between $63 million and $67 million, marking an 11-18% increase, following the implementation of Category I CPT codes for its Barostim therapy. The company also announced strong preliminary Q4 and full-year 2025 results, expanded its debt facility to $100 million, and received FDA approval for its BENEFIT-HF trial design. Analysts maintain a consensus "Buy" recommendation, indicating positive momentum despite the company's cash burn for growth initiatives.

feed.title: BNP Paribas Exane Adjusts KKR & Co Price Target to $160 From $162, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: BNP Paribas Exane has adjusted its price target for KKR & Co. to $160, down from $162, while reaffirming an "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst actions, including Rothschild & Co Redburn raising its price target to $167 and Barclays lowering its target to $159, both maintaining positive ratings. KKR & Co. is a financial services group primarily involved in insurance and asset management, with significant assets under management.

feed.title: DC’s Parent Company Sued by Paramount, Why Netflix Deal Might Not Happen
feed.summary: Paramount has filed a lawsuit against Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) seeking more information about WBD's potential deal with Netflix. Paramount is also trying to purchase WBD and is offering $30 per share, while Netflix's deal is for $27.75 in cash and Netflix stock for WBD's studio and streaming assets. The lawsuit and Paramount's intent to nominate directors and propose bylaw amendments aim to sway WBD shareholders towards their offer over Netflix's.

feed.title: Live Nation NZ Confirms Bank of New Zealand as Naming Rights Partner for Waikato Regional Theatre
feed.summary: Live Nation New Zealand has announced a five-year naming rights partnership with Bank of New Zealand (BNZ) for the Waikato Regional Theatre, which will now be known as BNZ Theatre. The 1,300-seat venue in Hamilton is set to reopen in January 2026, aiming to become a major cultural hub and drive economic benefits for the Waikato region. The theatre will host diverse programming, including performances by prominent artists like TEEKS, Sir Dave Dobbyn, Bic Runga, and Sophie Ellis Bextor.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $37,500 of AMGEN INC. lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: A recent Lobbying Disclosure Act filing revealed that $37,500 in lobbying efforts for AMGEN INC. were just disclosed for Q4 2025, primarily focusing on pharmaceutical industry issues. The article also details recent congressional and insider trading activity involving AMGN stock, as well as institutional holdings and analyst ratings, with a median price target of $318.0.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $90,000 of ANALOG DEVICES INC. lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: ANALOG DEVICES INC. (ADI) recently disclosed $90,000 in lobbying expenditures for Q4 2025, focusing on the CHIPS and Science Act and semiconductor industry policy. The article also details recent congressional stock trading, insider trading activity, and hedge fund movements related to ADI, alongside current analyst ratings and price targets for the company.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $40,000 of THE PROCTER & GAMBLE COMPANY lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: The Procter & Gamble Company (PG) recently disclosed $40,000 in lobbying expenditures for Q4 2025, focusing on OTC drug regulation, FDA, FTC, and healthcare policy. The article also details recent congressional stock trading activity in PG, with several representatives making purchases and sales, and significant insider selling by various executives. Furthermore, it covers institutional investor activity, with some increasing and others decreasing their stakes, along with recent analyst ratings and price targets for PG stock.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Highlights Procter & Gamble's Recent Rally
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Procter & Gamble's recent rally and discussed its performance within the consumer packaged goods sector, noting its resilience compared to weaker operators. He previously recommended PG as a company with a strong dividend history, suggesting a dollar-cost averaging strategy for investment due to its current undervaluation and potential for change with a new CEO. While Cramer acknowledges PG's value, the article also notes the potential for greater upside in certain AI stocks, specifically mentioning an undervalued AI stock benefiting from Trump-era tariffs and onshoring.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says “Cardinal Is Really the Monster” Among Drug Distributors
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Cardinal Health (NYSE: CAH) as a strong performer among drug distributors, referring to it as "really the monster" of the group. The company, along with Cencora and McKesson, saw over 40% growth last year. Brown Advisory also noted Cardinal Health's benefits from rising demand for specialty therapies and its strong execution in the medical distribution business.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: F5, Inc. (FFIV) Shareholders Who Lost Money
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP is alerting shareholders of F5, Inc. (FFIV) who suffered losses to contact them regarding a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit stems from two significant drops in F5's stock price following disclosures of a "highly sophisticated nation-state threat actor" gaining unauthorized access to company systems and subsequently providing low growth expectations for fiscal 2026. Investors who purchased F5 securities between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025, have until February 17, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: Gauzy Ltd. (GAUZ) Shareholders Who Lost Money Urged To Contact Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP About Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP is reminding investors of a class action lawsuit against Gauzy Ltd. (GAUZ) with a lead plaintiff deadline of February 6, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Gauzy made materially false and misleading statements during the Class Period between March 11, 2025, and November 13, 2025, concerning the financial health of its French subsidiaries which subsequently entered insolvency proceedings, causing a significant stock price drop. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm to learn more about recovering their losses.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $160,000 of HORMEL FOODS CORPORATION lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: A recent Lobbying Disclosure Act filing revealed that Hormel Foods Corporation (HRL) spent $160,000 on lobbying in Q4 2025, addressing issues such as "Meat & Poultry workers/immigration" and "Farm Bill." The article also details congressional stock trading activity in HRL, with Representative Lisa C. McClain making four trades in the past six months, alongside insider trading where Elsa A. Murano sold 13,400 shares. Furthermore, institutional investor activity shows significant additions and removals of HRL shares, and Wall Street analysts have issued mostly "Buy" or "Overweight" ratings with a median price target of $27.0.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Paychex: “People didn’t like the last quarter, I thought it was okay”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed Paychex, noting that while some people were critical of its last quarterly performance, he personally found it to be acceptable. His commentary suggests a divergence of opinion regarding the company's recent financial results.

feed.title: FTC Blocks Edwards Lifesciences’ $945M JenaValve Acquisition
feed.summary: The Federal Trade Commission has blocked Edwards Lifesciences' $945 million acquisition of JenaValve Technology, stating the deal was not in the public's best interest. Edwards Lifesciences disagrees with the decision and has revised its full-year 2026 adjusted earnings per share guidance downward as a result. The company plans to provide additional updates during next month's fourth-quarter earnings call.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says International Business Machines “Is Still Very Inexpensive”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted International Business Machines (IBM) as one of his "old tech" favorites, noting that it is still very inexpensive despite significant runs in its stock price. He mentioned that Cisco Systems, another legacy tech giant, has also shown strong performance. Cramer emphasized that both IBM and Cisco, which is part of his Charitable Trust, continue to perform well, with IBM trading at record highs but still considered cheap and a buy.

feed.title: Justices Seek US Views on General Dynamics’ No-Poach Suit
feed.summary: The US Supreme Court has requested the solicitor general's input on a case concerning "no-poach" agreements involving General Dynamics and other shipbuilders. The antitrust case, revived by a divided Fourth Circuit ruling, alleges an unwritten agreement since the early 1980s to not recruit each other's naval engineers, which purportedly suppressed wages and limited professional opportunities. A former naval engineer is representing thousands in this class action.

feed.title: Why Capital Bancorp is splitting CEO roles at bank and parent
feed.summary: Capital Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ: CBNK) has announced significant executive leadership changes, renewing CEO Ed Barry's employment and elevating Steve Poynot to CEO of Capital Bank, N.A. This restructuring aims to align the company's management with its long-term growth strategy as a diversified financial services enterprise. The company reported $3.4 billion in assets as of September 30, 2025.

feed.title: Co-Diagnostics announces 1-for-30 reverse stock split By Investing.com
feed.summary: Co-Diagnostics, Inc. (NASDAQ:CODX) will effect a 1-for-30 reverse stock split on January 2, 2026, to meet Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement. The company's stock has plummeted 69.44% year-to-date, and it reported significant revenue decline in Q3 2025. Despite financial challenges, Co-Diagnostics continues to expand its technology, recently securing an Australian patent for its Co-Dx PCR Pro platform and receiving a "Buy" rating from Maxim Group.

feed.title: PepsiCo's PFNA Struggles: Can Permissible Snacks Revive Volumes?
feed.summary: PepsiCo is facing challenges in its North American snack division (PFNA) due to changing consumer preferences and pressured spending. To combat this, the company is focusing on its "permissible snack" portfolio, which includes healthier options and refreshed legacy brands. Competitors Coca-Cola and Keurig Dr Pepper are also adopting similar "permissible" strategies within their beverage portfolios to stabilize volumes and cater to health-conscious consumers.

feed.title: Read This Before Buying JPMorgan’s Active Value ETF | JAVA
feed.summary: JPMorgan Active Value ETF (JAVA) employs active management to identify undervalued companies, charging 0.44% annually compared to 0.04% for passive value ETFs. The fund has outperformed the S&P 500 in 2026's volatile start but its 111% portfolio turnover creates tax friction from short-term capital gains. While suitable for investors seeking active value exposure, its higher fees, tax implications, and sector concentration risk make it less ideal for cost-conscious or income-focused retirees.

feed.title: DTE Energy Advances on Clean Energy Initiatives & Investments
feed.summary: DTE Energy is making significant capital investments to modernize its infrastructure, expand clean energy initiatives, and enhance service efficiency for customers. The company plans to invest $30 billion over the next five years, with a focus on renewable generation and energy storage, targeting 2,900 MW by 2042. Despite these growth catalysts, DTE faces financial risks due to its substantial long-term debt and challenges in its Energy Trading business.
